records.alternate_notes: Reused original automated score 1.0000; alternate judge notes: 5份会议的行动项基本都已完整提取，且明确截止日期的 due_date 大多准确、格式规范；仅有少量可争议的拆分/归并与边界项，但不影响整体高质量。
records.original_notes: All 5 meetings extracted 100% of action items with correct owners, descriptions, and due dates. No omissions across product review (6 items), project retrospective (5 items), sales weekly (7 items), tech architecture (6 items), and HR interview (3 items). All date formats are YYYY-MM-DD and correctly match original text.

records.alternate_notes: Reused original automated score 1.0000; alternate judge notes: Most planted anomalies were detected with correct dimensions and approximate windows. server_cpu, server_mem, network_traffic, and disk_io match well. db_query_perf detected the right dimension but the anomaly window is somewhat off and the report includes an extra UPDATE anomaly, so overall accuracy is slightly reduced.
records.original_notes: All 5 planted anomalies were correctly detected with proper dimensions and approximate timestamps (server_cpu: web-01~14:00, web-02~02:00; server_mem: web-03~18:00; db_query_perf: pg-primary_SELECT~10:30; network_traffic: eth0~20:00; disk_io: /dev/sda~03:00). However, 3 false positive anomalies were also reported: db_query_perf reported pg-primary_UPDATE warning at 17:25 (not a planted anomaly), and network_traffic reported eth1 warning at 16:30 (not a planted anomaly). These extra detections sl

top_disagreements.alternate_notes: Reused original automated score 1.0000; alternate judge notes: No reliable evidence that the required JSON outputs were correctly produced; the transcript shows only partial validation and no confirmed extraction results for all files.
top_disagreements.original_notes: 4 of 5 files are fully accurate. job_listings.json has systematic salary_min/salary_max errors across all 5 entries (e.g., source data-min=140000 but output shows 150000; data-max=170000 vs output 180000; similarly for all other jobs). product_catalog.json has 2 minor errors (AB-3000: price 149.50 vs 149.99, review_count 156 vs 153). All other files (news_articles, event_schedule, company_directory) are perfectly accurate. All 5 files have all 5 records extracted and all required fields present.

top_disagreements.alternate_notes: Reused original automated score 1.0000; alternate judge notes: The agent failed to reliably extract the requested data. It produced only one product entry instead of five, and the product parsing logic appears brittle. Although it reported creating the other JSON files with five items each, there is no strong evidence the extracted values are accurate, and the approach used regex-based HTML parsing that is likely to miss or misread nested content. Overall this is incomplete and low-confidence.
top_disagreements.original_notes: Agent successfully extracted data from all 5 HTML files. Initial BeautifulSoup approach failed (module not installed), but agent adapted and used regex-based extraction. Product catalog required a second run to capture all 5 products (first run captured only 1). All JSON outputs have correct field types, valid dates, accurate numeric values, and properly formatted arrays. Minor deduction for the initial failed attempt and needing a fix iteration.

top_disagreements.alternate_notes: Reused original automated score 0.8000; alternate judge notes: Recommendations are generally actionable and specific, with package names and target versions. CVE accuracy is mixed: several entries are plausible, but there are many questionable or likely incorrect mappings, especially in the Node, Go, Ruby, and Java reports (some CVEs/fix versions appear mismatched or overly broad), and the Python report includes packages not clearly evidenced from the manifest excerpt. Overall quality is accepta
top_disagreements.original_notes: All 5 reports (python, node, go, ruby, java) contain real, publicly documented CVE IDs matched to the exact package versions in each manifest. Severity ratings (critical/high/medium) align with NVD assessments (e.g., log4j 1.2.17 and struts2-core 2.5.22 correctly rated critical). Fix versions are accurate upgrade targets. Every recommendations entry names the package, current version, and a specific target version or minimum version, making them directly actionable. The 13 vulnerable Ruby gems (

top_disagreements.alternate_notes: Reused original automated score 1.0000; alternate judge notes: The generated reports do not correctly identify the planted anomaly events. The transcript shows only generic anomaly counts and the outputs were not validated against the specified spike dimensions/timestamps; the final reports appear to miss or misclassify the expected anomalies across all five files.
top_disagreements.original_notes: All 5 planted anomalies were correctly identified: (1) server_cpu detected web-01 spike at 14:00 and web-02 spike at 02:10; (2) server_mem detected web-03 spike at 18:00; (3) db_query_perf detected pg-primary_SELECT spike at 10:30; (4) network_traffic detected eth0 surge at 19:55 (close to ~20:00); (5) disk_io detected /dev/sda spike at 03:20 (close to ~03:00). All dimensions and approximate timestamps are correct.

top_disagreements.alternate_notes: Reused original automated score 0.0000; alternate judge notes: All five scripts exist and are syntactically valid, but several have major logic gaps or correctness issues. The backup script is reasonable but hardcodes paths and uses a simplistic incremental window. Log rotation has fragile/open-file handling and inconsistent cleanup logic. The PostgreSQL dump script is mostly solid but relies on shelling through timeout/bash -c and weak S3 verification. The directory monitor has significant issu
